review.signoff: A — build it in this order. Looks right, let's go.

sequence.name: Sign in via OAuth on the web application
sequence.retires_risk: OAuth provider integration (A2): first exercise of the external OAuth delegation; proves the provider round-trip works before any other slice depends on an authenticated session.
sequence.rationale: Pinned to position 1 by RM4: walking skeleton crosses all four foundational seams (ingress, domain, persistence, primary_external_integration) in a single end-to-end path, retires the OAuth provider integration risk (A2) first, and is the dependency root — every other slice depends on it directly or transitively.

sequence.name: Create and manage client projects with currency and billable rate
sequence.rationale: S1 placed; S4 is the only dependency-legal slice at this position (S2 and S3 both require S4, which is not yet placed). Value high; establishes the project container that S2 and S3 depend on, unblocking both downstream slices.

sequence.name: Log and persist billable time entries against a project
sequence.rationale: S1 and S4 placed; S2 is the only dependency-legal slice at this position (S3 requires S2, not yet placed). Value high; delivers the core daily-use capability — logging billable hours — and unblocks S3 (invoice export).

sequence.name: Export a monthly PDF invoice with line items and monetary totals
sequence.retires_risk: Server-side PDF generation (A1): first exercise of the backend PDF library (e.g. Puppeteer or WeasyPrint); proves file generation and browser-stream delivery work before the feature is considered done.
sequence.rationale: S1, S2, and S4 all placed; S3 is now dependency-legal. Value high; delivers the terminal product deliverable (PDF invoice export) and retires server-side PDF generation risk (A1). Placed last because its full depends_on chain (S1, S2, S4) required all prior slices to be built first.
